Release Notes.content:
Release: modOS v1.0.0 Cambaz

With the guide finally being done, I announce modOS v1.0.0 Cambaz.

Before I nerd out about everything added, here is more information about Mor ve Ötesi, the band behind Cambaz:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mor_ve_%C3%B6tesi

Why is modOS different?

Well, throughout the path of following the guide and making modOS, I completely rejected the idea of a hard-coded anything.

Sure, it might be open-source, but what is the point of open-source if changing the source is going to take many hours of coding?

modOS competes against a permanent webOS.

The following features highlight how modOS achieves that:

  • modOS does not have any hard-coded applications, bar, desktop, etc.
  • Every app is found in a JSON file (apps.json located in base/system/) which can be modified to install any application you want, easily! Currently this step is manual, and there is no official repository of verified applications. However, that is subject to change.
  • Bartender (the bar system of modOS) is a JavaScript file (bartender.js found in base/system/bartender.js) which can be modified to make the bar whatever you want!
  • WER (Window Manager for modOS) is a script that inserts window functions such as minimize and close buttons, alongside the window name, icon, and movement system. The whole script is launched with initwindow(win) where win is the main window element.
  • Got confused with initwindow(win)? Not to worry, modOS comes with the exec command: exec(appname, icon), where appname is required and icon is optional. The script (exec.js found in base/system/) looks through apps.json and loads the required content. Note: Custom icons will be overridden by the database icon to preserve consistency.
  • The desktop shows every app in apps.json for easy access. Just double click to launch. Note: Hidden applications exist. Use showhidden() and hidehidden().
  • Customize modOS via base CSS in config.css (found in /system/user/).

Default Applications List

  • App Template: Template for creating your own apps (hidden by default)
  • run: Developer console alternative for modern browsers or limited devices
  • Release Notes: Updated modOS news
  • Theme Song: Plays "Cambaz" by Mor ve Ötesi

Future Updates

  • Signup/login system for cross-platform sync (apps, settings, customizations). Optional guest mode remains.
  • modOS developer mode for building applications for the ecosystem.
  • Window minimization improvements. Currently windows minimize but cannot reliably be restored without document.querySelector(".win").style.display="block".
  • More applications via developer mode. Expansion is community-driven.

Theme Song.content: Cambaz - Mor ve Ötesi
Theme Song.script: function initAudioVisualizer(){const audio=document.getElementById("special-theme-song-audio");const canvas=document.getElementById("special-theme-song-canvas");if(!audio||!canvas)return;const ctx=canvas.getContext("2d");const audioContext=new AudioContext();const source=audioContext.createMediaElementSource(audio);const analyser=audioContext.createAnalyser();analyser.fftSize=256;source.connect(analyser);analyser.connect(audioContext.destination);const bufferLength=analyser.frequencyBinCount;const dataArray=new Uint8Array(bufferLength);audio.addEventListener("play",()=>{audioContext.resume();draw();});function draw(){requestAnimationFrame(draw);analyser.getByteFrequencyData(dataArray);ctx.clearRect(0,0,canvas.width,canvas.height);const barWidth=7;for(let i=0;i<bufferLength;i++){const value=dataArray[i];const height=value*0.5;ctx.fillStyle="#fff";ctx.fillRect(i*barWidth,canvas.height-height,barWidth-1,height);}}}
